item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It bugs me that the stone refers to "unknown dialect" when it ought to be a known dialect because they have the stone in their possession

item.title: Re: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
Noon until whenever at night generally, EST. I'm there more than I'm not, even if not actively chatting.

Also, the Sarkicism Hub has been tagged with daevite now!

item.title: Re: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded: I might take you up on that offer, but it will be some time (at least a week… probably a bit longer because I'm a slow writer). I'll need to take the feedback you've already provided and try some things out to rework my draft. In general, what times are you generally available on IRC chat?

item.title: Re: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
I feel that since the Sarkicism Hub is tied to Daevite lore so heavily, it should be tagged with "daevite".

It's less that the daevites are all over the sarkicism hub and more that the existence of Sarkicism is owed to the Daevites. Basically, Ion and most early Sarkics were Daevite slaves, and they rose up against their masters. In creating Sarkicism, Meta had to flesh out the Daevites as a civilization more than 140 did with its rather broad strokes. Now, there is no canon of course, so you could make them patriarchal if you wanted to, but in referencing Meta's work specifically, you're kind of telling users that you're operating within that universe.

As for the dates, I chose December 21st 1993 and February 28th 1994 because those are the dates that Don't Ask, Don't Tell was issued and instituted, respectively. I didn't see the two months as a long time because I've always seen The Foundation as bureaucratic and replacements need to be trained (Dr. Rahal is basically one of the only people fluid in the language).

This is a super fair explanation, and I get exactly what you intended, but know that it didn't immediately translate through the text.

As for reviews, if you want to pop in the IRC chat, I'd be happy to take a look at a draft over there.

item.title: Re: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
Well this sucks. I got some mildly glowing reviews before posting but I guess this one still needs some work.

I mostly agree with your criticism, although there are two parts that I'll write a bit about:

block
– hide block

When writing this SCP, I researched Daevite lore through exploring pages tagged with "daevite". I feel that since the Sarkicism Hub is tied to Daevite lore so heavily, it should be tagged with "daevite". I'm also not sure how expansion events tie in here (i.e. Daevite history is fluid and can change in an instant. How is this reconciled?).

As for the dates, I chose December 21st 1993 and February 28th 1994 because those are the dates that Don't Ask, Don't Tell was issued and instituted, respectively. I didn't see the two months as a long time because I've always seen The Foundation as bureaucratic and replacements need to be trained (Dr. Rahal is basically one of the only people fluid in the language). I could shorten the time to two weeks, although I find the difference to be arbitrary.

Based on the rating logistics there's a good chance that this SCP will die a slow death. So I think it'll be in my best interest to rewrite this article from scratch and post it in the same place (which I think is allowed).

Is there anyone interested in reviewing such a rewrite? In particular anyone with strengths in historical and/or dialogue centric SCPs? If so, please PM me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, I really like the hidden poem at the end!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hint: Highlight the blank white space at the end of the article.

Anyways, I liked the story. +1

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
Taking a look due to the disparate rating to comment ratio.

+ Crit
- Crit

If SCP-4824-1 becomes unwilling to continue residing near SCP-4824, SCP-4824-1 will be relocated to a temporary containment cell within Site-36, where it will be processed.

Upon a full read, I've noticed that this line never comes to pay off. If that's the case, why dedicate such a comparatively large portion of the containment procedures to it? It's ultimately a nitpick but still something to think about.

SCP-4824 is a sandstone obelisk located in the Gobi

The "Gobi" would not be how a scientific organization refers to the Gobi Desert for the first time. They would say the full name of the geological location, as well as the country that it resides in (since the Gobi is both in China and Mongolia).

that is precisely 10.42 meters in height and 16.78 meters in diameter

I don't think 4 significant digits is necessary here. Feels pedantic.

Visually, SCP-4824-1 is an adult man of good health.

If this guy is Daevite, then he wouldn't appear as an adult man, he would look like an adult Daevite as described by SCP-140. There are visual differences between man and Daeva.

All attempts to retrieve biological samples from SCP-4824-1 have failed as the samples will transmute into sand when removed.

Comma after "as".

For this reason, health diagnostics, such as SCP-4824-1's age, cannot be accurately determined. However, crosschecking SCP-4824's interview logs and SCP-4824-1's inscriptions with historical data suggests that it is more than two thousand years in age.

This information (SCP-4824-1 is 2000 years old) could be conveyed much more simply than this, and probably should be. We know he is Daevite and the Daeva have been extinct for thousands of years now.

SCP-4824-1 possesses geomancy

I don't think this is how the Foundation would express this ability, given that most 'magic' is explained with pseudoscientific terms like thaumaturgy, telekinesis, etc.

Are you sure you wouldn't rather hear the tale of Nadox and the Mekhanite?

I get why -1 would have liked Nadox, what with Nadox being an ostracized Daevite himself, but that tale occurred long after the collapse of the Daevite empire, far away from the Gobi Desert. That tale happened after the Late Bronze Age Collapse, after the fall of the Sarkic Deathless Empire and the disappearance of Prophet Ion. Doesn't really make sense to me how -1 would know what happened to Nadox after he joined Ion, honestly.

Some time ago, I was the only man in line for the throne. I denied the bride chosen by my kingdom… I chose a man instead.

So, a big thing about the Daevite lore according to Metaphysician's Sarkicism stuff is that the Daeva were matriarchal. It's actually pivotal to the origin story of Nadox, as "a Daeva priestess cut off his tongue, sewed shut his mouth, and had him emasculated (The complete removal of the penis and the testicles; an apparently common form of punishment among the matriarchal Daevites.)." As such, I find it really difficult to believe that this guy was a prince who was next in line for the throne, given that the Daeva would prefer a woman's rule. Yes, they would likely punish homosexuality severely, but I doubt the man would even be in the line of succession to begin with.

Dr. Rahal: You could come with me.

SCP-4824-1: Seth, I've dreamed about those words. Leaving this desert with you and entering untouched paradises… but such places don't exist.

Okay, this bit here gave me some serious narrative whiplash. We literally just established that -1 was gay like maybe 3 questions ago, and now the doctor is offering to run away with -1 just a few minutes later? I get that they may have interacted previously to learn the language and such, but this just feels like a total stretch to me.

starting on February 28th, 1994

Why was he given all of this extra time to be around an anomaly that he's on record as being infatuated with? That just seems like asking for trouble.


Overall, this isn't bad by any stretch, but the dialogue is pretty rocky and your Daevite lore is shoddy, which really takes me out of the piece. Unfortunately, it's a downvote from me.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to TheMightyMcB or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great to see this up officially!
I think now that you've made some changes it works really well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can agree with that.

Come to think of it, I'm not sure I could name any other skips that contain themselves as 4824 does. It's a lovely article whatever the object class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't seem to me that 4824 contains -1 at all, as -1 is stated to be capable of destroying 4824 at will.

I disagree with this logic. Just because SCP-4824 can be easily destroyed by SCP-4824 does not mean that SCP-4824 can't act as a containment unit.

-1 does not seem to contain 4824 so much as actively maintains its existence. This is not containment, it is perpetuation.

I do agree with your logic here. Thinking about it more, it is a jump in logic to view perpetuation as the same as containment.

With this in mind, I'll change the object class to Euclid for now.

Upon closer inspection, is there an established esoteric class SCP that contains itself? Looking at the guide, I suppose not. I find this surprising for some reason. But I don't care enough to make yet another esoteric class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't seem to me that 4824 contains -1 at all, as -1 is stated to be capable of destroying 4824 at will. It could leave, but chooses not to; this does not sound like Hiemal-type containment to me.

-1 does not seem to contain 4824 so much as actively maintains its existence. This is not containment, it is perpetuation. If removing the influence of -1 instead caused the sandstorm wall to expand uncontrollably and exponentially, then I could see justification for Hiemal; as the anomaly stands, it really does read like Euclid. -1 may leave anytime, chooses not to, and does not seem to be actively hostile.

From the Hiemal Class Discussion thread:

In essence, this class would be used for anomalies which, themselves, combat or suppress the effects of another anomaly. Containing or neutralizing the Hiemal class object results in that other anomaly surfacing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My justification is as follows:

  1. SCP-4824 contains SCP-4824-1 physically, with a sandstorm wall.
  2. SCP-4824-1 contains SCP-4824 through proper maintenance. SCP-4824 would been effectively neutralized from the passage of time a long time ago if not for SCP-4824-1's geomancy.

That said, I understand if people don't think this is sufficient for a Hiemal class (esoteric classes tend to be particularly ridged in their definitions in my experience). However, I personally find it poetic, because to me it implies that SCP-4824-1 effectively and efficiently contains himself.

If you believe that it should be some other object class (ex. Euclid), let me know why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is your justification for the Hiemal class here? It reads to me as if 4824-1 is the primary anomaly, with the obelisk and attendant weather phenomena being a creation of -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading my Series-5 Contest entry!
It's not too late to enter… right?

Special thanks to these reviewers:

  • Helichopper__
  • Corvidless
  • UraniumEmpire
  • LittleFieryOne
